jokes.text: Hi, I'm April. I stole your wallet.
jokes.setup: Meeting an NFL player in a social setting, everyone being polite
jokes.punchline: April casually admits to theft as an introduction
jokes.explanation: Perfect April character moment - the deadpan delivery and casual criminality paired with social introduction creates unexpected comedy. High character integration.

jokes.text: You guys are weird. Thank you.
jokes.setup: NFL player calls them weird after wallet theft comment
jokes.punchline: April takes it as a compliment instead of an insult
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency - April embracing weirdness. Simple but effective subversion of normal social response.

jokes.text: Keep walking, 98. Skill positions only for Donna Meagle.
jokes.setup: NFL linebacker trying to talk to Donna
jokes.punchline: Donna dismisses him because he's not a skill position player
jokes.explanation: Great Donna moment showing her high standards and sports knowledge. The specificity of 'skill positions only' makes it funnier than generic pickiness.

jokes.text: "Run"? Tommy doesn't run. That's your guys' job. Now, uh, let's go fetch this football throw, fellas.
jokes.setup: Question about running in slippers during NFL event
jokes.punchline: Tom's complete misunderstanding of sports participation plus 'football throw'
jokes.explanation: Classic Tom - treating athletics like a business meeting. 'Football throw' is a nice malapropism showing his sports ignorance.

jokes.text: This morning I saw a YouTube clip of a little puppy riding a motorcycle. So my bar for "stunning" is pretty high.
jokes.setup: Leslie calling the reservoir stunning
jokes.punchline: Tom's completely different definition of stunning involving internet animals
jokes.explanation: Solid character moment showing Tom's internet-addled attention span and different priorities from Leslie's nature appreciation.

jokes.text: Then I would tell you the puppy had a little side car with a piggy in it named Hampton.
jokes.setup: Leslie mentions fluoride, Tom continues his puppy tangent
jokes.punchline: Specific absurd detail about the pig in a sidecar
jokes.explanation: Continues the absurd escalation. The name 'Hampton' adds specificity but it's somewhat random humor.

jokes.text: Pawnee is going to have fluoride in its water for the first time in our history, our oral history. Nailed it.
jokes.setup: Serious discussion about fluoride and town history
jokes.punchline: Leslie's proud reaction to her terrible oral/dental pun
jokes.explanation: Classic Leslie - proud of a mediocre pun. The self-satisfaction ('Nailed it') makes it funnier than the pun itself.

jokes.text: Lame duck? Try flame duck... 'cause this duck is on fire.
jokes.setup: Leslie addressing her lame duck status
jokes.punchline: Forced rhyme and fire metaphor with over-explanation
jokes.explanation: Typical Leslie wordplay that's more forced than clever. The over-explanation kills any wit, but it's in character.

jokes.text: Oh, my God. He's not wearing any pants. What's wrong with him? Let's get out of here. He's smiling at us! Let's go! Run!
jokes.setup: Tom pointing out a cheerful trash collector
jokes.punchline: Reveal that the cheerful worker is pantsless
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag subversion - what seemed like a positive example becomes horrifying. The escalating panic is well-executed.

jokes.text: Several months ago, I was on a walk and found my crib tree. I approached the tree, murdered it, left it to cure in my woodshop
jokes.setup: Ron explaining his handmade crib
jokes.punchline: Describing tree harvesting as 'murder' in Ron's matter-of-fact way
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron voice - the dramatic word 'murdered' for cutting down a tree, delivered with complete seriousness. Great character integration.

jokes.text: Are you going to crawl my way? By Lenny Kravitz.
jokes.setup: Chris discussing parenting books
jokes.punchline: Confusing 'Are You Gonna Go My Way' with a parenting book title
jokes.explanation: Good Chris moment showing his tendency to mix up references. The baby/crawling connection makes the confusion logical.

jokes.text: I am confident that it is literally the safest crib in the explored universe.
jokes.setup: Chris defending his store-bought crib
jokes.punchline: Absurd confidence and scope ('explored universe')
jokes.explanation: Classic Chris hyperbole. 'Explored universe' is a nice touch showing his tendency to overstate everything.

jokes.text: Counteroffer-- you take me nowhere, and I talk to no one.
jokes.setup: Chris suggesting they visit the crib store
jokes.punchline: Ron's complete rejection of social interaction framed as a business negotiation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron line - anti-social preference delivered with business formality. The structure of 'counteroffer' makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Counter-counteroffer-- you come with me, or I make you attend a four-hour fax cover-sheet protocol meeting.
jokes.setup: Ron's refusal to go anywhere
jokes.punchline: Chris threatening the most boring government meeting possible
jokes.explanation: Good escalation continuing the negotiation format. The specific bureaucratic torture is believable and funny.

jokes.text: Fluoride can control minds? Like, you can use it to make ladies do stuff?
jokes.setup: Discussion about fluoride mind control conspiracy
jokes.punchline: Jeremy's immediately thinking about using it on women
jokes.explanation: Typical Jeremy creepiness. The immediate jump to manipulating women is characteristically awful but predictable.

jokes.text: How am I the one that is getting booted off of this city council?
jokes.setup: Jeremy asking about using fluoride to control women
jokes.punchline: Leslie's exasperation at being recalled while Jeremy stays
jokes.explanation: Good meta-commentary on the absurdity of politics. Leslie's genuine frustration makes the irony work.

jokes.text: I don't want fluoride in the water because I'm a dentist, and I like cavities.
jokes.setup: Jamm explaining his opposition to fluoride
jokes.punchline: Admitting his corrupt financial motive with fake casualness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jamm - transparently corrupt but admitting it like it's reasonable. The casual delivery makes the awful motive funnier.

jokes.text: Got an Asian girl to sit in it once.
jokes.setup: Jamm bragging about his expensive car
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate racial comment and implied sex work
jokes.explanation: Characterizes Jamm as gross but it's lazy shock humor. The racism feels cheap rather than clever satire.

jokes.text: Remember last year when no one would get flu shots 'cause there was a rumor they turned you European?
jokes.setup: Discussing people's irrational fears about medical treatments
jokes.punchline: Absurd specific fear about becoming European
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist humor about American paranoia. 'Turned you European' is perfectly ridiculous while feeling believable for Pawnee.

jokes.text: Some lardos in Muncie ate tainted Sweetums toffee sticks and diarrhea'd so much, they ended up in the hospital.
jokes.setup: Jessica reporting corporate crisis
jokes.punchline: Casual callousness toward poisoned customers
jokes.explanation: Shows Jessica's corporate sociopathy. 'Lardos' and casual discussion of mass poisoning reveals her character.

jokes.text: I want to throw up and kill myself.
jokes.setup: Ben commenting that the adoption dogs are cute
jokes.punchline: April's extreme negative reaction to cuteness
jokes.explanation: Perfect April - violent overreaction to cute animals delivered deadpan. The extremity makes it funny rather than concerning.

jokes.text: If you can look at that site and not want to take home 50 dogs, you're basically a war criminal.
jokes.setup: Discussing the cute dog adoption website
jokes.punchline: Extreme comparison between not wanting dogs and war crimes
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from cute dogs to war crimes. The hyperbole works because it captures the manipulative power of cute animal websites.

jokes.text: Larry, I swear to God, if you leave right now, we will make your spirit dog a miniature schnauzer. Is that bad? Yes!
jokes.setup: April threatening Larry if he leaves for the airport
jokes.punchline: Larry doesn't understand why a miniature schnauzer would be bad
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Larry's obliviousness to the insult and April's exasperated confirmation. The threat itself is petty and perfect.

jokes.text: You bought a machine that replicates an open window?
jokes.setup: Chris mentioning their 'Sounds of Nature' machine
jokes.punchline: Ron's incredulous summary of the pointless purchase
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron logic - cutting through consumer nonsense to expose the absurdity. The economy of words makes it hit harder.

jokes.text: I'd like to solve the puzzle of parenting.
jokes.setup: Chris looking for the 'Vanna White book on child care'
jokes.punchline: Mixing Wheel of Fortune with parenting book shopping
jokes.explanation: Classic Chris confusion mixing TV game shows with parenting. The earnestness makes it funnier than if it were intentional.

jokes.text: Oh, yeah, that thing is a deathtrap.
jokes.setup: Chris proudly asking to show off the 'amazing crib'
jokes.punchline: Store employee immediately calling it a deathtrap
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection - completely undercuts Chris's confidence instantly. The casual delivery of 'deathtrap' makes it funnier.

jokes.text: It burned off my fingernails.
jokes.setup: Explaining the dangerous chemicals in the crib
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of horrific personal injury from the product
jokes.explanation: Good dark escalation. The casual delivery of extreme injury continues the pattern of understated horror.

jokes.text: You definitely understand what chemicals are, Perd.
jokes.setup: Perd agreeing with Jamm's nonsensical chemical list
jokes.punchline: Jamm's sarcastic 'praise' for Perd's obvious ignorance
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Perd's gullibility. Jamm's condescending sarcasm while manipulating him is characteristically awful.

jokes.text: I'm like a white, male U.S. senator.
jokes.setup: Leslie realizing she has no consequences as a lame duck
jokes.punchline: Comparing herself to the most privileged political position
jokes.explanation: Sharp political satire with perfect timing. The specificity of 'white, male U.S. senator' makes the privilege comparison pointed and funny.

jokes.text: but now the binders are bright yellow! Boom!
jokes.setup: Leslie planning to fight Jamm with same tactics
jokes.punchline: Her idea of extreme rebellion is yellow binders
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie moment - her idea of going rogue is still very bureaucratic. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: What's he gonna be in ten years--Snoop Laser Snake?
jokes.setup: Tom explaining Snoop Dogg's name changes as rebranding example
jokes.punchline: Absurd future name prediction
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist escalation following the logical pattern of Snoop's names. 'Laser Snake' is perfectly ridiculous.

jokes.text: Oh, no! That is insane! I will burn this place to the ground if you pick that one!
jokes.setup: Someone choosing Craig as their spirit dog
jokes.punchline: Craig's extreme violent overreaction to the comparison
jokes.explanation: Perfect Craig - completely disproportionate emotional response. The threat of arson over dog comparison is peak Craig hysteria.

jokes.text: Because you're pretty and you like makeup and stuff.
jokes.setup: April explaining why she picked a specific dog for Donna
jokes.punchline: Shallow reasoning that misses Donna's actual complexity
jokes.explanation: Sets up the later payoff with Tom's better analysis. April's surface-level observation will be contrasted with deeper insight.

jokes.text: You have used 9. I have used 20. Continue.
jokes.setup: Ron's rule about 100-word conversations
jokes.punchline: Keeping exact count and treating it like a game show
jokes.explanation: Great character consistency - Ron's efficiency taken to logical extreme. The meta-aspect of counting words while speaking adds cleverness.

jokes.setup: Chris continuing to talk after reaching the word limit
jokes.punchline: Ron ending the conversation exactly at his 100-word limit
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to the word-counting setup. The economy is flawless - one word that ends the scene and delivers the laugh.

jokes.text: fluoride, chemical, tiny genitals, misinformation, panic, death, Jenny McCarthy.
jokes.setup: Jamm giving his anti-fluoride presentation
jokes.punchline: Random list of scare words ending with Jenny McCarthy
jokes.explanation: Good satire of fear-mongering tactics. The inclusion of Jenny McCarthy as the final scare word is a nice 2013-era reference.

jokes.text: What the-- what just happened?
jokes.setup: Tom's dramatic water demonstration
jokes.punchline: Confused reaction to whatever Tom just did
jokes.explanation: Basic reaction to visual gag we can't see. Gets the job done but nothing special about the line itself.

jokes.text: Welcome to T-dazzle. It's not a chemical. It's an aquatic-based social-media oral experience.
jokes.setup: Tom rebranding fluoride
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous marketing speak for water with fluoride
jokes.explanation: Great Tom moment - combining his business knowledge with his tendency toward over-complicated marketing. 'Aquatic-based social-media oral experience' is perfectly ridiculous.

jokes.text: Someone like Ron is teaching even when they're not teaching. Does that make sense? My anxiety has kept me up for over 50 hours.
jokes.setup: Chris explaining how he learns from Ron
jokes.punchline: Immediate contradiction showing his obvious sleep deprivation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chris - philosophical insight immediately undercut by obvious personal crisis. The juxtaposition makes both parts funnier.

jokes.text: We call it "Drink-ems"! Whoo!
jokes.setup: Jessica proposing Sweetums take over water supply
jokes.punchline: Childish branding with inappropriate celebration
jokes.explanation: Shows Jessica's tone-deaf corporate enthusiasm. The exclamation and 'whoo!' are inappropriately cheerful for a municipal takeover.

jokes.text: Did you bring those with you just to do that? Maybe. Damn. It is a baller move.
jokes.setup: Jamm presumably doing something dramatic with chess pieces
jokes.punchline: Tom's begrudging respect for the planned dramatic gesture
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Tom appreciating showmanship even from an enemy. The grudging admission makes it funnier.

jokes.text: You're beautiful, yet cold and aloof. You pride yourself on being a loner. You do not obey, you choose to cooperate. And when you stop baring your fangs to pick a mate, it's for life. And you're fiercely loyal to your pack, which makes you a rare black Siberian husky.
jokes.setup: Tom analyzing Donna after her criticism of his shallow earlier assessment
jokes.punchline: Incredibly detailed and accurate character analysis through dog metaphor
jokes.explanation: Exceptional character writing - Tom reveals hidden depth while perfectly capturing Donna's personality. The callback structure and psychological insight make this a standout moment.

jokes.text: That was so spot-on, it was scary. I need to go lie down for 45 minutes. No, an hour... a full hour.
jokes.setup: Tom's incredibly accurate analysis of her personality
jokes.punchline: Donna's overwhelmed reaction and escalating need for recovery time
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to Tom's analysis. Donna's need for increasing recovery time shows how deeply he got to her.

jokes.text: What's next-- cookie dough toothpaste? Bad example. That would be amazing.
jokes.setup: Leslie making an argument about Sweetums ruining health with bad products
jokes.punchline: Immediately undermining her own argument by admitting the example sounds great
jokes.explanation: Great Leslie moment - her love of sweets overriding her political argument. The immediate self-correction is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Because you were embezzling... I hope.
jokes.setup: Ben getting fired, Leslie asking why
jokes.punchline: Leslie hoping it was for something actually bad rather than her actions
jokes.explanation: Dark but effective - Leslie would rather Ben be a criminal than suffer for her actions. The hopeful delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: What do you want, Leslie? I love him, and I will do anything to help him get his job back. What do you want?
jokes.setup: Leslie saying she'll do anything to help Ben
jokes.punchline: Jessica immediately cutting to the chase, bypassing emotional manipulation
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jessica's transactional nature. She immediately gets to business rather than engaging with emotion.

jokes.text: You are such an autumn.
jokes.setup: Jessica demanding Leslie wear lime-green because it will look horrible
jokes.punchline: Jessica's condescending fashion analysis
jokes.explanation: Shows Jessica's shallow cruelty through fashion. The seasonal color analysis adds specific detail to the insult.

jokes.text: 1970s funk and 1770s opera and Sugar Ray, post Mark McGrath after he left for Entertainment Tonight.
jokes.setup: Tom asking about April's favorite music
jokes.punchline: Extremely specific and contradictory music tastes
jokes.explanation: Perfect April - deliberately confusing and contradictory answers. The specificity of 'post Mark McGrath' makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Favorite book? Downton Abbey. That's not a-- Downton... Abbey.
jokes.setup: Tom asking about favorite books
jokes.punchline: April insisting a TV show is a book through repetition
jokes.explanation: Good April stubbornness - refusing to acknowledge she's wrong by just repeating herself more slowly.

jokes.text: In order-- Natural Born Killers, Remains of the Day, and Bill & Ted's Bogus Journey. Excellent.
jokes.setup: Asking about favorite movies
jokes.punchline: Completely random movie selection ending with Bill & Ted quote
jokes.explanation: Great April randomness - violent movie, period drama, comedy sequel make no sense together. The 'Excellent' callback is a nice touch.

jokes.text: Rad Dads by Apolo Anton Ohno and Shaun White.
jokes.setup: Chris mentioning previous parenting book
jokes.punchline: Completely inappropriate parenting book authors
jokes.explanation: Good Chris moment - taking parenting advice from Olympic athletes shows his tendency to trust any published advice.

jokes.text: That trash can is the toilet, and the wood was the feces.
jokes.setup: Chris trying to find parenting metaphors in Ron's woodworking
jokes.punchline: Extremely literal and gross metaphor interpretation
jokes.explanation: Shows Chris's desperate search for meaning. The potty-training metaphor is both gross and perfectly wrong.

jokes.text: That's why my favorite book is Moby Dick-- no froufrou symbolism, just a good, simple tale about a man who hates an animal.
jokes.setup: Ron saying he hates metaphors
jokes.punchline: Completely missing the obvious symbolism in Moby Dick
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Ron moment - completely missing the most symbolic book in American literature. The earnestness makes it even funnier. This is exceptional character writing.

jokes.text: You're not a dog. You're a cat.
jokes.setup: Tom struggling to find April's spirit dog
jokes.punchline: Realizing she's not a dog at all
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection - breaks the rules of the game to make the perfect point. The simplicity after all the analysis makes it hit harder.

jokes.text: What will we do-- sleep all day, take cooking classes together, sit on a couch and watch movies? God, that sounds horrible.
jokes.setup: Leslie saying they can't both be unemployed
jokes.punchline: Describing paradise as if it's torture
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie - treating relaxing activities like punishment because of her workaholic nature. The sarcasm reveals character.

jokes.text: Each Drink-ems is packed with 35% of your daily monopotassium phosphate requirement. MPP is an emulsifier as well as a plus-7 PH buffer. Now, I know what you're thinking-- what about its molar mass? How does 136.086 grab you?
jokes.setup: Leslie giving her apology speech
jokes.punchline: Overwhelming the audience with fake technical jargon
jokes.explanation: Good Leslie strategy - using her policy wonk tendencies to make Sweetums' product sound boring through technical overload.

jokes.text: Well, miss, I think we'd all appreciate it if you just swallowed a teaspoon of hush.
jokes.setup: Leslie trying to continue her boring technical speech
jokes.punchline: Tom's polite but firm shutdown with drink metaphor
jokes.explanation: Good Tom line - maintaining his smooth presentation style while telling Leslie to shut up. The drink metaphor ties to the water theme.

jokes.text: I'm like Picasso meets Michael Jordan. I can paint, but, uh, I can also be a world-class gambler.
jokes.setup: Tom bragging about his diverse talents
jokes.punchline: Confusing basketball Jordan with gambling movie character
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Tom's earlier Michael Jordan mix-up, showing his continued confusion about celebrities.

jokes.text: I'm like Picasso meets Michael Jordan. I can paint, but, uh, I can also be a world-class gambler.
jokes.setup: Tom explaining his diverse talents
jokes.punchline: Confusing Michael Jordan with someone known for gambling
jokes.explanation: Classic Tom confusion mixing up Jordan's basketball skills with his gambling problems. Shows his tendency to misunderstand references.

jokes.text: There's more than one crib tree in a forest. That's not a lesson, by the way, just a comment on lumber availability.
jokes.setup: Ron explaining why he can give away the crib he made
jokes.punchline: Explicitly clarifying it's not a metaphor to avoid Chris's misinterpretation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron callback to Chris's metaphor obsession. The clarification that it's literal shows Ron learning to prevent misinterpretation.

jokes.text: Does the white whale actually symbolize the unknowability and meaninglessness of human existence? No. It's just a shitty fish.
jokes.setup: Chris asking about Moby Dick symbolism
jokes.punchline: Ron's continued literal interpretation and dismissal
jokes.explanation: Great callback to the Moby Dick joke. 'Shitty fish' is perfect Ron - crude but definitive rejection of literary analysis.

jokes.text: I think so. I'll double-check with Tom, but I'm, like, 95% sure.
jokes.setup: Ben asking if H-2-flow is actually fluoride
jokes.punchline: Leslie's casual uncertainty about what they just put in the water supply
jokes.explanation: Good ending beat - Leslie's confidence in Tom's rebranding despite not being entirely sure what they've done. Shows the chaos of their solution.
